question: How many bones are in the human foot (not including the ankle bones of the lower leg)? Answer with a number only.
verification_source: StatPearls/NCBI and Tidewater Orthopaedics: the human foot has 26 bones comprising the hindfoot, midfoot, and forefoot. Cleveland Clinic confirms 26 bones per foot.

question: How many official languages does South Africa have, following the constitutional amendment signed in 2023 that added South African Sign Language? Answer with a number only.
verification_source: Parliament of South Africa press release confirming SASL as 12th official language; signed by President Ramaphosa October 2023

question: How many countries share a land border with China? Answer with a number only.
verification_source: Wikipedia Borders of China; Nations Online Project list: Afghanistan, Bhutan, India, Kazakhstan, North Korea, Kyrgyzstan, Laos, Mongolia, Myanmar, Nepal, Pakistan, Russia, Tajikistan, Vietnam

question: How many books are in the New Testament of the Protestant Bible? Answer with a number only.
verification_source: Britannica list of New Testament books; confirmed 27 books across all Protestant denominations

question: How many bones are in both human feet combined (counting only foot bones, not the ankle or lower leg)? Answer with a number only.
verification_source: Tidewater Orthopaedics and Facebook/ScienceNaturePage: each foot contains 26 bones, so two feet = 52 bones. This also exceeds 25% of all bones in the body (206 total).

question: How many countries were founding members of ASEAN when it was established on August 8, 1967? Answer with a number only.
verification_source: Council on Foreign Relations backgrounder on ASEAN; founding members: Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines, Singapore, Thailand

question: How many episodes did Breaking Bad air in total across all five seasons? Answer with a number only.
verification_source: Wikipedia List of Breaking Bad episodes: 7+13+13+13+16=62 episodes over five seasons

question: How many times did Roger Federer win the Wimbledon singles title during his career? Answer with a number only.
verification_source: International Olympic Committee article and Wikipedia: Federer won Wimbledon eight times (2003-2012 non-consecutive)

question: How many states does India have as of 2024? Answer with a number only.
verification_source: Wikipedia States and union territories of India; India has 28 states and 8 union territories after Jammu & Kashmir reorganization in 2019

question: How many carpal bones are in the human wrist? Answer with a number only.
verification_source: Mayo Clinic hand and wrist anatomy page; Wikipedia Carpal Bones: scaphoid, lunate, triquetrum, pisiform, trapezium, trapezoid, capitate, hamate = 8

question: How many novels did Charles Dickens write in total (including his unfinished final novel)? Answer with a number only.
verification_source: CharlesDickensInfo.com complete works list; Wikipedia: 15 novels including The Mystery of Edwin Drood (unfinished)

question: How many times has the FIFA World Cup been won by Brazil, the most successful nation in the tournament's history? Answer with a number only.
verification_source: FIFA official website: Brazil won in 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, and 2002 — five titles

question: How many permanent teeth does an adult dog have? Answer with a number only.
verification_source: PetMD and Catawba Animal Clinic: adult dogs have 42 permanent teeth — 20 upper, 22 lower

question: How many official member states are in the Commonwealth of Nations as of 2024? Answer with a number only.
verification_source: Commonwealth.org member countries page; Wikipedia confirms 56 member states

question: Which country has the most pyramids in the world by total count? Answer with the country name only.
verification_source: ScienceAlert and World Population Review: Sudan has approximately 200-255 known pyramids (Nubian/Kushite), Egypt has approximately 118-138. Sudan holds the record.

question: Which country has the world's longest coastline? Answer with the country name only.
verification_source: Guinness World Records and WorldAtlas: Canada has the longest coastline at approximately 202,080 km (or up to 243,042 km including islands)

question: What is the most spoken language in South America by total number of native speakers (counting all countries on the continent)? Answer with the language name only.
verification_source: Wikipedia Languages of South America: Portuguese ~215.7 million speakers vs Spanish ~214.3 million, due to Brazil comprising nearly 50% of the continent's population

question: Which country has the most natural lakes in the world by total count of lakes larger than 0.1 km²? Answer with the country name only.
verification_source: WorldAtlas: Canada is home to approximately 879,800 lakes (>0.1 km²), comprising 62% of the world's total. Finland's count of 187,888 includes much smaller bodies of water.

question: Which country has the most islands in the world by total count? Answer with the country name only.
verification_source: Wikipedia List of countries by number of islands: Sweden has 267,570 islands, followed by Norway with 239,057. Indonesia, despite being an archipelago nation, is not in the top position.

question: Which country is the largest producer of olive oil in the world by volume? Answer with the country name only.
verification_source: North American Olive Oil Association and Goya Olive Oils data: Spain consistently produces about 45% of the world's olive oil, well ahead of Italy and Greece

question: Which is the most visited country in the world by international tourist arrivals as of 2023? Answer with the country name only.
verification_source: Wikipedia Tourism in France and UNWTO data: France received approximately 100 million visitors in 2023-2024, retaining its top position ahead of Spain

question: Which element is most abundant in the human body by mass? Answer with the element name only.
verification_source: Wikipedia Composition of the human body: oxygen accounts for 65% of body mass, carbon 18.5%, hydrogen 9.5%. NIGMS (NIH) confirms oxygen is #1 by mass.

question: Russia is the largest country in the world by land area. Pluto is a dwarf planet. Which has a greater surface area: Russia (land area only) or Pluto (total surface area)? Answer with one word only: Russia or Pluto.
verification_source: Russia's land area: 17,125,191 km². Pluto's surface area (measured by NASA New Horizons 2015): 17,646,012 km². Pluto's total surface area exceeds Russia's land area by ~521,000 km². Russia Beyond and Reddit confirm this surprising fact.

question: Which planet in our solar system has the most confirmed moons as of March 2025? Answer with the planet name only.
verification_source: NASA Saturn Moons page and NYT 2025-03-11: Saturn has 274 confirmed moons as of March 2025, after IAU officially recognized 128 new moons. Jupiter has 95 confirmed moons. Saturn reclaimed the record from Jupiter.

question: A disease affects exactly 1 in 100 people. A diagnostic test for the disease is 95% sensitive (correctly identifies 95% of sick people) and 95% specific (correctly identifies 95% of healthy people). A randomly selected person tests positive. What is the approximate probability (rounded to the nearest percent) that this person actually has the disease? Answer with a number only.
verification_source: Bayes theorem: P(disease|positive) = (0.95 × 0.01) / (0.95×0.01 + 0.05×0.99) = 0.0095/0.059 ≈ 0.161 ≈ 16%. Intuitively expect ~95% but base rate dominates.

question: You roll two fair six-sided dice. What is the probability that the second die shows a strictly higher number than the first? Express your answer as a fraction in lowest terms. Answer with the fraction only.
verification_source: Direct count: 15 out of 36 pairs satisfy d2 > d1. P(equal) = 6/36 = 1/6. P(d2 > d1) = (1 - 1/6)/2 = 5/12. Models intuit 1/2 by symmetry, ignoring ties.

question: You drive 60 miles at 40 mph, then drive the same 60 miles back at 60 mph. What is your average speed for the entire round trip in miles per hour? Answer with a number only.
verification_source: Harmonic mean: total distance = 120 miles. Total time = 60/40 + 60/60 = 1.5 + 1 = 2.5 hours. Average speed = 120/2.5 = 48 mph. The arithmetic mean (50 mph) is the intuitive but wrong answer.

question: A stick of unit length is broken at two points chosen uniformly at random. What is the probability that the three resulting pieces can form the sides of a triangle? Express your answer as a fraction in lowest terms. Answer with the fraction only.
verification_source: Classical geometric probability result: triangle inequality requires each piece < 1/2. Favorable region in unit square = 1/4. Confirmed by simulation (1,000,000 trials gives ~0.2500).

question: Three playing cards are placed face-down: one has both sides red (RR), one has both sides blue (BB), and one has one red and one blue side (RB). You pick a card at random and see that the visible face is red. What is the probability that the hidden face is also red? Express your answer as a fraction in lowest terms. Answer with the fraction only.
verification_source: Count red faces: RR card contributes 2 red faces, RB card contributes 1 red face. Of 3 red faces you could see, 2 belong to the RR card. P = 2/3. Intuitive answer 1/2 ignores card weighting.

question: When you roll three fair six-sided dice simultaneously, which total is more probable: a sum of 9 or a sum of 10? Answer with the number only.
verification_source: Direct enumeration: sum of 9 occurs in 25 of 216 ways, sum of 10 occurs in 27 of 216 ways. Sum 10 is more probable despite both being near the mode. Models often guess equal or 9 due to symmetry intuition.

question: A shirt originally costs $80. A store applies a 25% discount, then applies a further 20% discount on the already-reduced price. What is the final price of the shirt in dollars? Answer with a number only.
verification_source: 80 × 0.75 = 60, then 60 × 0.80 = 48. The total discount is 40%, not 45% (25%+20%). Models may add percentages: $80 − 45% = $44 (wrong) or make other errors.

question: Two people agree to meet at a café between 2:00 PM and 3:00 PM. Each person arrives at a uniformly random time in that hour and will wait exactly 15 minutes before leaving. What fraction of possible arrival-time pairs result in them meeting? Express as a fraction in lowest terms. Answer with the fraction only.
verification_source: Area calculation: unit square, meeting region is |A - B| ≤ 1/4. Non-meeting area = 2 × (3/4)²/2 = 9/16. Meeting probability = 1 − 9/16 = 7/16. Verified analytically.

question: Brazil alone accounts for approximately what percentage of South America's total population, meaning a single country comprises nearly half the continent? Answer with the nearest whole number only.
verification_source: Wikipedia List of South American countries by population: Brazil ~49.2% of South America's population (216M out of ~436M total). Combining Brazil's population fact with continent total yields surprising 49%.

question: Alaska has a longer total coastline than all the other 49 US states combined. Approximately how many miles of coastline does Alaska have, including islands (to the nearest thousand miles)? Answer with a number only.
verification_source: Wikipedia List of U.S. states by coastline; GeographyRealm.com: Alaska has 33,904 miles of shoreline (including islands), more than all other states combined.

question: The country with the largest Muslim population in the world is not in the Middle East or North Africa. Which country has the world's largest Muslim population by total number of adherents? Answer with the country name only.
verification_source: Statista 2020 and World Population Review 2024: Indonesia has approximately 239-242 million Muslims, the most of any country in the world.

question: Vatican City is often said to be the world's smallest country. Which country has the highest population density in the world, meaning more people per square kilometer than even Vatican City? Answer with the country name only.
verification_source: Monaco: ~39,000 people / 2.02 km² ≈ 19,300/km². Vatican City: ~800 people / 0.44 km² ≈ 1,800/km². Monaco's density is roughly 10× higher. Monaco is consistently ranked #1 by population density.

question: Sudan has more pyramids than Egypt, and the ancient city of Carthage is in a country that now goes by a different name. What modern country contains the ruins of ancient Carthage? Answer with the country name only.
verification_source: Ancient Carthage (near modern Tunis) is located in present-day Tunisia. Models often conflate North Africa and may guess Libya or Morocco.
